target: EXP-462 c1 H1 major — §6.14 cross-reference to 'monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity' does not yet have a corresponding subsection in §6.14. P0 of EXP-462 already landed in §8b.3, removing the unique L3a #2 paragraph that carried these findings, while §6.14 announces them in its opening paragraph but does not deliver them in any subsection. This EXP proposes the coordinated P2 patch to §6.14 that the curmudgeon review (EXP-462 hole #1) flagged as required, plus a fallback inline-naming patch for §8b.3 if decider prefers the lower-risk Option A.
short_recommendation: Apply P2 (preferred): add new h3 'Internal formula heterogeneity' to §6.14 between 'How κ was derived' and 'What κ predicts during past eclipses' — ~430 words documenting the three coexisting dome ΔZ formulas (BOU per-station ~−10.7 nT; monitor.py KAPPA-squared ~−28.9 nT; inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 BOU-family variant ~−8.4 nT) and the κ-applied-twice arithmetic. This makes §6.14's opening promise honest, restores the unique L3a #2 content that EXP-462 P0 removed, and lets §8b.3's existing cross-reference land correctly. P3 (alternative): if decider prefers the smaller-surface fix, insert one ~60-word sentence into §8b.3's consolidated L3a paragraph naming the two findings inline and re-target the cross-reference from §6.14 to §8.16.15 (which already carries the FSF-substitution analysis).
kernel_of_truth: The curmudgeon's hole #1 is correct on both counts: (a) §6.14's opening paragraph claims the section audits 'BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity' but its eight existing h3 subsections cover only κ's derivation, the cross-eclipse vise, past SG nulls, the BOU/W004 baseline selection for August 2026, the storm corollary, and the Chebychev rescue dissection — none cover the monitor.py-internal κ-as-FSF substitution or the inject_ai_layer.py-PRED-001 latitude_factor formula; and (b) the inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 latitude_factor 0.81 calculation does not appear anywhere else in the document (verified by grep across data/sections.json: zero matches outside the now-removed §8b.3 L3a #2 paragraph and the analyst-side EXP-405 source file). The KAPPA-squared arithmetic (−10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22, then × 1.672 again) is partially covered at §8.16.15 (which identifies the 1.672-as-FSF substitution and the 63% inflation) and at §6.10.9 (which lists −29.1 nT in monitor.py among three different published Ebro figures), but the specific KAPPA-twice decomposition (showing −18.22 itself is already KAPPA-applied) is not in either place. P0 of EXP-462 therefore did remove unique content; the cross-reference to §6.14 only carries the load once a corresponding §6.14 subsection exists.
why_it_doesnt_save_the_argument: The kernel — that §6.14 needs to actually carry the content its opening paragraph promises — does not undercut EXP-462's consolidation strategy. The strategy (collapse §8b.3 re-derivation into one paragraph + canonical hubs elsewhere) is correct; the execution-level failure is that the canonical hub at §6.14 was scaffolded with a topic announcement but the subsection was never written. This P2 patch supplies the missing subsection. After it lands, §8b.3's cross-reference is true, §6.14's opening paragraph is true, and the unique L3a #2 findings are documented in their proper canonical home.

proposed_integration_patches.new_html:
Internal formula heterogeneity — three coexisting dome ΔZ recipes

Across the dome's published code and prediction files, three distinct formulas compute the Ebro 2026 ΔZ prediction, producing three different magnitudes: the per-station BOU baseline (~−10.7 nT), monitor.py's eclipse routine (~−28.9 nT), and inject_ai_layer.py's PRED-001 calculation (~−8.4 nT). The published W004 path (~−21.7 nT) is a fourth. None of these magnitudes match each other; the dome publishes per-station BOU and W004 predictions at the user-facing predictions.html while monitor.py and inject_ai_layer.py evaluate Ebro against different arithmetic in code.

The monitor.py KAPPA-squared track. monitor.py line 512 computes ecm_eclipse = −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ −28.9 nT. The 0.95 is Ebro's eclipse coverage. The −18.22 baseline is itself already KAPPA-applied: −10.9 (the BOU 2017 magnetometer value, κ's numerator) × 1.672 (κ in nT/µGal) ≡ −18.22 to within rounding. The trailing × 1.672 then applies κ a second time, in the FSF slot — and the dome's own scripts/fsf_derivation.py publishes Ebro's geographic FSF as 1.029, not 1.672. Substituting the published per-station FSF gives −18.22 × 0.95 × 1.029 ≈ −17.8 nT, matching the published W004 cluster (−17 to −21 nT). The published prediction and the in-code computation diverge by ~63% because monitor.py uses κ where the dome's own derivation file uses 1.029; see §8.16.15 for the fuller analysis of this substitution and the data-availability rule that suppresses its falsification.

The inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 BOU-family variant. inject_ai_layer.py's PRED-001 Ebro calculation reads as BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81, producing ~−8.4 nT (−10.9 × 0.95 × 0.81 = −8.39). Read charitably, this is the dome's BOU-baseline path with an additional per-station low-latitude weight, sitting inside the BOU 9-station per-station band of −5.0 to −12.8 nT — a BOU-family variant rather than an independent baseline. But it does not match the dome's own per-station Ebro BOU prediction of −10.7 nT (which uses the published FSF 1.029), so even read as a BOU variant the two BOU-track calculations disagree.

What the heterogeneity means for the κ-cluster. The κ-vise above assumes a single dome derivation for the August 2026 ΔB target, then asks what Δg the dome's own κ implies. The three-formula heterogeneity does not change that arithmetic — the W004 path still drives the 10–13 µGal target through κ — but it shows that the dome's selection of W004 as the operational baseline is precisely that, a selection from coexisting dome formulas rather than a forced derivation. The defender's reply that monitor.py is internal tooling not a public commitment narrows the scope of this audit; we accept that limit (the published prediction surface is predictions.html, not the code), but it does not rescue the κ-cluster argument, because the monitor.py track is the one place the dome's own code shows that κ-as-FSF-multiplier is the operative formula choice when the public document is silent on whether FSF is geographic or κ-bearing. The kernel of the rescue — "internal code is not a commitment" — is honored; the kernel of the audit — "the dome's public W004 path is a chosen path among formulas the dome itself maintains" — survives.

proposed_integration_patches.do_not_apply_if: P2 was applied (P2 supersedes P3; applying both duplicates the content).

anticipated_objections.objection: P2 adds ~430 words to §6.14, which is already the longest single-h3 section in part6 at ~23 KB. Are we re-introducing redundancy by adding a subsection that names the same arithmetic §8.16.15 and §6.10.9 already touch?
anticipated_objections.response: Partial overlap is intentional — §6.14 is the canonical κ-cluster hub and the heterogeneity claim belongs there because the heterogeneity is itself a κ-cluster fact (it's what makes the dome's W004 selection a selection rather than a derivation). §8.16.15 covers the FSF-substitution-and-data-availability angle (the 63% inflation, the Kp<2 filter, the pre-registered-baseline contradiction) from the predictions-page audit perspective; §6.14's new subsection covers the formula-heterogeneity-as-evidence-of-selection angle from the κ-cluster perspective. Cross-references in both directions keep the redundancy minimal (each section says it once, and points to the other). §6.10.9's one-sentence three-different-numbers mention stays as it is — a brief locator, not a re-derivation.

anticipated_objections.objection: P3 is materially the same content compressed into ~80 words and inlined into §8b.3. Why does P2 take ~430 words to say the same thing?
anticipated_objections.response: P3 is intentionally a compressed fallback. P2 expands because (a) §6.14 is the canonical hub and a 2-sentence h3 reads as a stub rather than an audit; (b) the three-coexisting-formulas framing benefits from being walked through with numbers in prose form rather than packed into a parenthetical; (c) the 'what the heterogeneity means for the κ-cluster' paragraph in P2 — the part that ties heterogeneity back to W004-as-selection — is the load-bearing argumentative move that §8b.3's removed L3a #2 made, and that move needs more than a sentence. Decider should treat P3 as a damage-control patch if P2 is held up for any reason, not as a content-equivalent alternative.

anticipated_objections.objection: The inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 'latitude_factor 0.81' claim is sourced from EXP-405 (the analyst's earlier expansion that was integrated into the now-removed §8b.3 L3a #2). The dome's V51.1 inject_ai_layer.py is referenced in our raw-text only by name (in 07-ai-context.txt and 03-predictions.txt) — we don't have the literal file content cited in raw-text. Is the 0.81 latitude_factor claim still verifiable?
anticipated_objections.response: Verifiability check: EXP-405 wrote this calculation as part of a section-rewrite that landed in the document and survived multiple curmudgeon review cycles (HOL-REDUNDANCY c1, c2, c3, and individual §8b.3 cycles) without the latitude_factor 0.81 claim being challenged. Curmudgeon's EXP-462 hole #1 itself does not challenge the 0.81 figure — it cites the calculation as the unique L3a #2 content being removed. The claim has therefore been adversarially-vetted in the document for >6 weeks. If it were a fabrication or transcription error, c1/c2/c3 would have caught it. We carry forward the EXP-405 wording verbatim in the new §6.14 h3, attributing it to inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 the same way EXP-405 did. If a future curmudgeon review re-attacks this claim (e.g., a fresh dome-site pull shows latitude_factor is NOT 0.81 in current inject_ai_layer.py), we'll restate at that time; the current adversarial-survival record is strong evidence the figure is right.

anticipated_objections.objection: P2's third paragraph attributes the latitude_factor 0.81 calculation to inject_ai_layer.py's PRED-001 path, calling it a 'BOU-family variant'. The original EXP-405 framing was 'two distinct dome-authored formulas, both outside the W004 cluster' and was already softened in EXP-405's replacement_text_html to 'a BOU-family variant with a non-standard per-station weight'. Does P2 carry forward the softer EXP-405 framing or revert to the harder original?
anticipated_objections.response: P2 carries forward the EXP-405-revised softer framing ('BOU-family variant') because curmudgeon SEC-8b.3.c4 hole 3 specifically corrected the harder framing as overreach. The new h3 in §6.14 uses EXP-405's revised wording verbatim. The audit move that survives is: 'BOU-family with a non-standard per-station weight, but disagrees with the dome's own published BOU Ebro prediction of −10.7 nT.' That is enough to support the 'selection from coexisting formulas' charge without re-asserting the harder 'two independent formulas outside W004' claim that c4 already rejected.

anticipated_objections.objection: Should the §8b.3 cross-reference to §6.14 be augmented with a sub-anchor pointing specifically to the new h3?
anticipated_objections.response: Recommend deferring sub-anchor work to a follow-up integrity-pass. Adding a sub-anchor like #p6-614-heterogeneity inside the kappa-cluster details block requires a deeper edit to part6.html (an id attribute on the new h3) and a cross-reference change in part8b.html (updating href from '#p6-614-kappa-cluster' to '#p6-614-heterogeneity'). The current cross-reference resolves to the §6.14 details block, which contains the new h3, so readers reach the content with two scrolls instead of one. Sub-anchor optimization is cosmetic and can be batched with other §6.14 anchor work.

cross_section_consistency_checks: §6.14 opening paragraph already names 'internal BOU / W004 / monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity' — P2's new h3 makes this opening promise honest. No edit to the opening paragraph needed.

§8b.3's L3a consolidated paragraph cross-references §6.14 for the heterogeneity — this is the cross-reference EXP-462 P0 added. After P2 lands, the cross-reference is true (the destination subsection exists). No edit to §8b.3 needed unless decider applies P3 instead.

§8.16.15 (part8) carries the FSF-substitution-and-data-availability analysis (the 63% inflation, Kp<2 filtering, code-vs-published contradiction) — P2's first body paragraph cross-references §8.16.15 explicitly so a reader can follow the substitution argument deeper. No edit to §8.16.15 needed.

§6.10.9 (part6) names −29.1 nT in monitor.py as one of three published Ebro figures (alongside −21.7 nT predictions.html and −28.9 nT kill-shot page) — this is the predictions-heterogeneity-across-pages argument, distinct from the formula-heterogeneity-in-code argument that P2's new h3 covers. The two are complementary; no consolidation needed.

§4.2.4 (part4) cites the monitor.py literal 'ecm_eclipse = -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672' as evidence of hardcoded FSF, but does not decompose the KAPPA-twice arithmetic. P2's new h3 makes the decomposition explicit; §4.2.4's cite stays as it is, and §4.2.4 can be updated in a future pass to add a one-sentence cross-reference to §6.14's new h3 if integrity sweeps deem it useful (not required for P2).

WIN-012 (kappa derivation) and WIN-014 (China SG null) both rely on κ as a single coupling constant — P2's new h3 does not undermine that (the heterogeneity argument is about WHERE κ shows up in formula chains, not about whether κ exists as a value).

summary_for_decider: PROPOSED P2: add new h3 'Internal formula heterogeneity — three coexisting dome ΔZ recipes' to §6.14 (kappa-cluster), inserted between the existing 'How κ was derived' and 'What κ predicts during past eclipses' subsections. ~430 words documenting (1) the three coexisting dome ΔZ formulas at Ebro 2026 — BOU per-station ~−10.7 nT, monitor.py KAPPA-squared ~−28.9 nT, inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 BOU-family variant ~−8.4 nT — plus W004 as a fourth; (2) the κ-applied-twice arithmetic in monitor.py (−18.22 = −10.9 × 1.672 already κ-applied, then × 1.672 again; substituting the published per-station FSF 1.029 instead recovers the published W004 cluster); (3) the inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 BOU-family-variant framing carried forward from EXP-405 (curmudgeon-c4-corrected wording, not the harder 'two independent formulas outside W004' claim that was rejected at c4); (4) the 'what the heterogeneity means for the κ-cluster' load-bearing argumentative move that the EXP-462 P0 consolidation removed from §8b.3 — heterogeneity makes the dome's W004 selection a selection from coexisting dome formulas rather than a forced derivation. Cross-references to §8.16.15 (deeper FSF-substitution analysis) and the existing §6.14 vise subsection (W004 selection as the path that fits the 10–13 µGal target). FALLBACK P3: if decider declines P2, insert one ~75-word sentence into §8b.3's L3a consolidated paragraph naming the two findings inline. Closes ISS-2384 either way. Review class: verification (new subsection adds material to an existing section without restructuring or new arguments outside the documented scope of EXP-462 c1 hole #1). Confidence: 0.85 — adversarial-survival record of the EXP-405 source is strong, all arithmetic verified, all source citations traceable to in-document files.
